fields.description:
## Why
Bug pelt-mead-aeon showed the review bot can serve /health 200 (pure liveness, app.py:113-116) while every review silently fails (the .tickets-tracker runner-config regression). Because autodeploy.sh's HEALTH_URL health gate (line 48) only probes /health, ADR-0026 auto-rollback never fired during a functional outage — the bad build stayed live. The root-cause fix (change 264) is landed, but this defense-in-depth gap remains: a functional failure is invisible to the deploy/rollback layer.

## What
- Add a functional readiness endpoint (e.g. /ready) to the review-bot app that exercises the review path wiring (ticket-store clone materialization + runner config re-root) — NOT just process liveness.
- Point autodeploy.sh's post-deploy health gate at /ready so a review-path regression triggers ADR-0026 auto-rollback to :prev.
- Keep the VOTER_ERROR CloudWatch alarm as the slower backstop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] /ready (or equivalent) exercises the review path and fails when ticket-store/runner wiring is broken
- [ ] autodeploy.sh gates the deploy on /ready; a broken build auto-rolls-back to :prev
- [ ] Test/proof that a simulated review-path failure trips the gate
